findings.claim: VAD speech routed through TurnArbiter at voice-agent-session.ts:571
findings.found: Line 571 is `const interruptedContextId = this.latestActiveTtsContextId();` (the active-TTS guard). The actual routing into the arbiter, `this.turnArbiter.onSpeechStarted(pkt, interruptedContextId)`, is at line 577.
findings.correction: Routing through TurnArbiter is at packages/voice/src/voice-agent-session.ts:577 (onSpeechStarted); line 571 is the latestActiveTtsContextId() gate.

findings.claim: TurnArbiter gates sustained speech + primary-speaker match before emitting interrupt.detected at turn-arbiter.ts:150
findings.found: Line 150 is `private tryCommit(nowMs: number): void` — it runs the minInterruptionMs gate (line 153) and primary-speaker gate (line 156), but the interrupt.detected emission itself is `this.emitInterruptDetected(pending.interruptedContextId)` at line 183, which calls `emitInterruptDetected` (defined at line 100) pushing `make.interruptDetected(...,"vad")` on Route.Critical.
findings.correction: Gate logic is at turn-arbiter.ts:150 (tryCommit) but interrupt.detected is emitted at line 183 via emitInterruptDetected (defined line 100). Cite 183/100 for the emission.

summary: Bridge's picture of current Syrinx barge-in state is substantially accurate. The Critical-before-Main bus drain (pipeline-bus.ts:23,276), interrupt routing through TurnArbiter, interrupt.detected handling (voice-agent-session.ts:848), outbound playout clear on interrupt.tts (outbound-playout-pipeline.ts:83), Cartesia cancel (voice-tts-cartesia/src/index.ts:188) and Deepgram CLEAR (voice-tts-deepgram/src/index.ts:145), and AI-SDK spoken-prefix history rewrite (voice-bridge-aisdk/src/index.ts:333) are all present as described. All absence claims hold up under grep: no onset_to_media_silent_ms / onset_to_logic_cancel_ms metrics (only suppressed_non_primary, suppressed_short_speech, committed_after_ms, latency_ms, gate_resolved_after_tts_end exist); no interrupt.started/interrupt.media_silent/interrupt.false_positive packets; no pause/resume of playout (resume tokens found are session-reconnect, not audio pause); no interruptible/uninterruptible frame taxonomy on PacedPlayoutFrame; clear() takes no args. Backchannel/semantic classification exists (voice-turn-pipecat/src/semantic-completeness.ts) but is wired to end-of-turn endpointing (index.ts:220,268), NOT the barge-in interruption gate — so the gap claim is correct. tts.playout_progress packet exists and is emitted server-side and consumed by recorder/turn-metrics, but voice-client-browser/src/audio.ts emits no playout progress and has no spoken-prefix/interrupt handling, so the 'browser not just telephony' gap is correct. All WBS-cited target files (packets.ts, turn-arbiter.ts, paced-playout.ts, outbound-playout-pipeline.ts, voice-client-browser/src/audio.ts, voice-server-websocket/src/index.ts, voice-bridge-aisdk/src/index.ts) exist. Two citation-precision MISCITEs only (line numbers off for the interrupt.detected emission and the TurnArbiter routing call); no build-wrong-thing or skip-needed-work errors. minInterruptionMs default is 280ms (voice-agent-session.ts:208) — bridge does not state a number so no conflict.
